title: The Villagers Resist Below
description: On pages 11–13, the villagers resist below as luffy reaches Dr. Kureha’s castle and meets Chopper, while the people below continue resisting Wapol’s return.
scene: The Villagers Resist Below. Luffy reaches Dr. Kureha’s castle and meets Chopper, while the people below continue resisting Wapol’s return. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee he

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Looks like your fever's gone down quite a bit, missy. You happy, puppy?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: And you are...?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm Dr. Kureha,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But you can just call me "doctress."

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Doctor? Then this is...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You want to know the secret of my youth?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: No, I never asked.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: This is the castle on top of the mountain.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: There should have been two people with me! Do you know where they are!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Yeah, they're sleeping soundly in the room next door. Real tough boys, those two.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Here, look.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Ah-

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: This here's the cause of your fever.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You were bitten by a Kestia, a poisonous tick that lives in the tropics.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: The bacteria living in that tick changes hosts when the tick sucks on the blood of another animal.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Once inside, the bacteria causes great pains for its new host for 5 days.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Fevers of 40 degrees or more and inflammation of various body parts occurs, including the myocardium, artery, and even the brain!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Judging by the bite mark, it looks like today was your third day since infection.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It must've been quite painful but the pain would've naturally subsided after 5 days of infection even if we left your body as it was... Hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Because in just 2 more days,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You'd be resting in peace in the after- world.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: ... What!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's why it's also known as the "S days disease." From what I've heard, Kestia should have went extinct over a hundred years ago,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So you're real lucky that I happened to have the antibiotic for it.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Just what the hell have you been doing, missy?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Running around half- naked on a prehistoric island?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Hee hee hee hee hee hee hee.. I'm just teasing Wi-

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Ah!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What, did I actually hit close to the mark? Well, aren't you a hopeless case.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Just go back to sleep for now. You haven't fully recovered yet.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: OOF!

summary: This part centers on the villagers resist below, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
